name: Bunyip
primary_sources: Aborigines of Victoria by Robert Brough Smyth

distinctive_features: seal-like body with shaggy black or brown coat
long neck with small head resembling a horse or emu
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: seal-dog variety with round head and whiskers; long-necked variety with elongated neck and horse-like tail

domain_specific_visual.water_dwelling: inhabits lakes, rivers, swamps, and billabongs

secondary_functions: predator of humans in some legends

domain_specific_functional.cultural_teaching: warns against overfishing and approaching water bodies carelessly
domain_specific_functional.ecological_role: symbolizes the dangers of water bodies

relationships.description: Represents the consequences of taking more than one's fair share from nature

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: southeastern Australia, particularly Victoria and New South Wales
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: embodies care for country and its people, teaching practical survival means
primary_sources: Aborigines of Victoria by Robert Brough Smyth
